title: quercus suber
intro: Quercus suber, also known as the cork oak, is a deciduous tree species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its thick, protective bark that peels off every 10-20 years to be harvested for cork production. The medicinal properties of Quercus suber include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its ability to reduce cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Quercus suber include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections.
